name: /standards-remove: warns when removed source is the sole provenance entry for >=1 active rule (orphans those rules; suggests S-7 promote alternative)
command:
mkdir -p tree/google && printf "source:
  id: my-source
  authoritative_publisher: M
  authoritative_url: https://my.com
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-04T00:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:S
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - {id: g-x-001, name: x, description: x, detector: x, type: problem, fixable: null, has_suggestions: false, deprecated: false, replaced_by: [], docs_url: https://x.com, requires_type_checking: false, recommended: true, options_schema: {}, messages: {default: x}, severity: P1, version: 1.0.0, semver: 1.0.0, rule_state: warn-only, provenance: [{source: my-source, class: published-standard, section_id: 1, url: https://x.com, fetched_at: 2026-05-04T00:00:00Z, content_hash: sha256:S}]}
recommended_set: [g-x-001]
all_set: [g-x-001]
" > tree/google/x.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-remove.sh" --id my-source --tree "$PWD/tree" --check-orphan-rules 2>out.txt && grep -E "g-x-001.*orphaned" out.txt 1>&2 && echo ok 1>&2
setup:
mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro && printf "- id: my-source
  name: My
  url: https://my.com
  tier: 1
  applies_to: [typescript]
  fetch_frequency: daily
" > .claude-tdd-pro/STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
